math_recheck: Re-derived the full chain symbolically+numerically: partial-wave optical theorem sigma_tot=(4pi/k^2)sum(2l+1)Im a_l (verified against integral of |f|^2), unitarity Im a_l>=|a_l|^2 from |1+2i a_l|<=1, Lehmann decay exp(-l sqrt(t0)/k) from z0=1+t0/2k^2, crossover L=(k/sqrt t0)N ln s, final sigma<=(4pi N^2/t0)ln^2 s = (pi/m_pi^2)ln^2 s; both code snippets run and the two ceilings agree; cross-checked semiclassical Yukawa argument and amplitude bounds (s ln^2 s fwd, s^{3/4} ln^2 s fixed-angle) against the Arkhipov 'Harmony' review and Martin-Roy.
fixes: context.md:13-25 fixed inconsistent amplitude normalization: paired sigma=(4pi/k)Im F with F=(sqrt s/4k)sum a_l P_l (off by sqrt(s)/4); switched to standard f=(1/k)sum a_l P_l so sigma=(4pi/k)Im f(0)=(4pi/k^2)sum(2l+1)Im a_l is internally consistent
context.md:27-31 renamed Mandelstam-rep amplitude A(s,t,u)->M(s,t,u) and F->f to remove notational clash with the s-channel absorptive part A
reasoning.md:3,7,9-12,54 same normalization fix (F->f, prefactor (sqrt s/4k)->(1/k)) keeping the final bound unchanged
reasoning.md:44 corrected tail estimate: claimed L*k/sqrt(t0)~L^2; it is actually L^2/(N ln s), so the tail is parametrically subleading by a factor of ln s (conclusion strengthened, not weakened)

reasoning.md:58-66 + code comment: corrected b_max=L/k from wrong (N/2)(1/sqrt t0)ln s to (N/sqrt t0)ln s (the 1/2 should cancel against k=sqrt(s)/2; code already used the correct value); restated disk area as pi b_max^2~(pi N^2/t0)ln^2 s matching the rigorous (4pi N^2/t0)ln^2 s only up to an O(1) disk-vs-sphere geometric factor instead of falsely claiming exact equality
answer.md:9,15,21 normalization fix (f with (1/k); flagged the |F|<~s ln^2 s statement as the distinct relativistic F normalization dsigma/dOmega=|F/sqrt s|^2)
answer.md:39 corrected b_max factor and clarified the O(1) geometric-factor caveat
answer.md:79 fixed typo '4pi/t0*N^2 (=4pi*4~12.57)' -> coefficient is 4pi (=4pi/t0*N^2 with t0=4,N=2)~12.57
